items.title: 3 held over fatal shooting outside Johor entertainment centre
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have detained 3 suspects, including a woman, to assist investigations into a fatal shooting outside an entertainment centre in Taman Industri Jaya, Skudai, over the weekend. Johor deputy police chief Hoo Chu Han said the trio, aged between 26 and 39, were arrested in the state capital on Sunday as part of ongoing investigations. Earlier, a 27-year-old woman alerted police after shots were fired outside the premises.

items.title: Education ministry mulls stationing security personnel at schools following fatal crashes
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Education Ministry will consider a proposal to station security personnel at all schools to bolster safety following two fatal car crashes at schools over the past month. Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the School Safety Management Guidelines were being finalised to improve existing protocols. Asked about the need for People's Volunteer Corps (Rela) members to be deployed at schools as a safety measure, Fadhlina said the ministry would study the proposal first.

items.title: Ringgit ends higher against regional peers, mixed versus majors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed higher against regional peers and was firmer versus most major currencies today as investors monitored developments in the United States-Iran negotiations and upcoming US-China talks. At 6pm, however, the ringgit eased against the US dollar to 3.9220/9260 from 3.9185/9230 at last Friday's close. The local currency also traded higher against regional peers.

items.title: Transport Minister flags lorry lane congestion at Second Link, issue to be raised with Home Ministry
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 11 — The issue of congestion involving heavy vehicle lanes on the Malaysia-Singapore Second Link at the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), which is alleged to be due to mandatory scanning inspections, will be brought to the attention of the Ministry of Home Affairs, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. Loke said that matters related to KSAB, including the operation of scanning machines, fall under the jurisdiction of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS), which is under the Home Ministry’s purview. The Ministry of Transport will provide feedback to the Home Ministry,” he said.

items.title: 'Rest N Go' massage chair operator offers 126.1mil new shares ahead of listing
items.summary: The company mainly engaged in providing vending massage services under the "Rest N Go" brand. The IPO involves the issuance of 126.1 million new shares, a 16 per cent of the enlarged issued share capital, as well as an offer for sale of 78.8 million offer ahares, about 10 per cent of the enlarged issued share capital. For the offer shares, 23.6 million will be allocated to selected Bumiputera investors approved by the ministry via private placement, while the remaining 55.2 million shares will be placed to selected investors.

items.title: Prosecution moves to seize Range Rover and gold jewellery from ex‑Army Chief’s wife
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — The prosecution has filed an application to forfeit a Range Rover Sport SVR vehicle and jewellery seized from the wife of former Army Chief Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan, Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin. The prosecution filed the application at the High Court here on May 4, naming 27-year-old Salwani Anuar as the respondent. The prosecution is seeking to forfeit to the government a Range Rover Sport SVR, a 916-gold bracelet weighing 15.10 grammes, and three gold rings weighing 1.33 grammes, 1.41 grammes and 1.35 grammes respectively.

items.title: Zahid: Umno remains relevant as more youth sign up online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno is receiving a positive signal as more young people choose to join the party through its online membership registration platform, said its president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the trend proves that the party, which is now 80 years old, continues to move in step with the times and remains trusted as an important platform in defending the country's stability and future. "This signals that Umno continues to move in line with changing times and is still trusted as an important platform in safeguarding the country's stability and future," he said in a Facebook post today in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary celebration.

items.title: NCT Group unit sells 40ha NSIP land for possible site by a global data centre operator
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: NCT Alliance Bhd's unit NCT World Sdn Bhd has signed a term sheet for the proposed sale of a 40-hectare land within the NCT Smart Industrial Park (NSIP). NCT Alliance said the land has been identified by a global data centre operator as a potential site for development, reflecting Malaysia's growing appeal as a regional hub for digital infrastructure. The interest also indicates the strength of NSIP's Managed Industrial Park (MIP) model, where integrated infrastructure, centralised estate management and long-term master planning create a stable and well-managed environment for large-scale, high-value investments.

items.title: 11 elephants, including calf, freed from abandoned pond in Kinabatangan
items.summary: KINABATANGAN: Eleven elephants, including a calf, were rescued after becoming trapped in an abandoned water pond near the Deramakot Forest Reserve at the FGV Cepat Ringgit B plantation here. The statement said all the Borneo pygmy elephants were rescued by a wildlife team deployed to the site to carry out the operation. The Borneo pygmy elephant is the smallest subspecies of Asian elephant, found only on the island of Borneo, mainly in Sabah, Malaysia.

items.title: Lorry congestion at KSAB to be raised with Home Ministry, says Loke
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Issues involving lorry congestion at the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) in Tanjung Kupang, Iskandar Puteri, will be raised with the Home Ministry, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. He said operations at the Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complex, including the use of scanners, fall under the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA). It is something we are constantly monitoring, and the Transport Ministry will provide feedback to the Home Ministry," he said.

items.title: Thunderstorms and strong winds to lash 10 states, MetMalaysia urges public to stay alert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to affect 10 states until 9pm today. Similar weather is also expected in Pahang, affecting Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Kuantan and Pekan; Selangor (Hulu Selangor); Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin); and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru). In Sarawak, the affected areas include Samarahan (Simunjan), Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa, Betong and Saratok), Sarikei, Sibu (Kanowit), Kapit (Song and Belaga), Miri (Beluru, Telang Usan, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 11, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: The ringgit weakened to three point nine two one zero against the US dollar. In the crypto market, Bitcoin slipped to three hundred seventeen thousand four hundred fifty nine ringgit. Ethereum and Solana also eased to nine thousand one hundred sixty three ringgit and three hundred seventy two ringgit, respectively.

items.title: 60,000 B40 students to get free motorcycle licences under MyB2S
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Some 60,000 Form Four and Form Five students from B40 families will receive free motorcycle licences under the 2026 MyLesen B2 Sekolah (MyB2S) programme. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said RM18.5 million has been allocated for the initiative this year. He said the programme was introduced following concerns over the high number of students riding to school without valid licences.

items.title: 6 arrested as Johor police dismantle 2 drug trafficking syndicates
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor police have dismantled two drug trafficking syndicates in separate operations last week, arresting six suspects and seizing drugs and assets worth more than RM4.8 million. Also seized were luxury vehicles, including a BMW, and RM213,770 in cash. Police seized various drugs, including methamphetamine, ketamine, Eramin 5 pills and ecstasy, with an estimated value of RM3.65 million.

items.title: Oil rises after Trump rejects Iran's response to US peace proposal
items.summary: Brent crude futures climbed US$2.70 or 2.67 per cent to US$103.99 a barrel at 0902 GMT. US West Texas Intermediate was at US$97.66 a barrel, up US$2.24, or 2.35 per cent. They rose to US$105.99 and US$100.37 a barrel, respectively, earlier in the session.

items.title: Bersatu expels Kok Lanas and Parit leaders, suspends Mersing MP and Bukit Bunga rep in latest disciplinary action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Bersatu’s Disciplinary Board today expelled and suspended four more elected representatives and party leaders with immediate effect. Those expelled were Kok Lanas assemblyman and Ketereh division chief Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi, as well as Parit division Armada chief Wan Meor Safwat Naqiuddin Shamsuddin. Meanwhile, Mersing MP Muhammad Islahuddin Abas and Bukit Bunga assemblyman Mohd Almidi Jaafar, who is also Jeli division chief, had their memberships suspended for two party election terms.

items.title: Urban T20 earners question fairness of proposed RON95 subsidy rationalisation
items.summary: On targeted subsidies, she said the move was unfair as T20 earners were among the country's largest contributors to income tax. Not all T20s are wealthy households," she said. she said.

items.title: Penang opposition questions CM Inc's unaudited accounts since 2009
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The state opposition has questioned Chief Minister Incorporated's (CMI) financial transparency, alleging the state-linked entity has never tabled complete audited accounts since 2009 despite managing millions in revenue. Fauzi, who is also chairman of the Penang Public Accounts Committee (PAC), said no comprehensive audited accounts had been made available to enable transparent scrutiny of the entity's financial management. CMI operates under a public-private-professional partnership model (4P) to maximise the strategic value of state assets, particularly land, and buildings.

items.title: Umno records over 16,800 new members in 20 days, 71pc youth, says Asyraf Wajdi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 11 — Umno managed to record 16,867 new members through its online registration platform within 20 days of its implementation, said the party’s secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. He said Umno took note and recognised the effectiveness of the party’s online registration platform for new members. Zahid was accompanied by Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and the party’s top leadership.

items.title: Orkim raises capacity with latest vessel, to acquire another this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Marine transportation company Orkim Bhd has received delivery of its latest vessel, Orkim Garnet, bringing its fleet size to 19, and increasing its total load capacity to 253,036 deadweight tonnes (dwt). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the group said Orkim Garnet, formerly known as Rich Sunda, is a 2026-built International Maritime Organisation (IMO) Type II tanker with a deadweight capacity of 13,850 tonnes (mt). Orkim chief executive officer Captain Cheah Sin Bi said Orkim Garnet enables the group to expand into chemical and palm oil transportation, allowing them to capture broader regional trade opportunities.

items.title: Public split over RON95 fuel subsidy cuts for T20 group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians remain divided over the government's move to rationalise RON95 fuel subsidies, with some arguing that assistance should prioritise lower-income groups. Azlan Senan, who frequents Bangsar Shopping Centre, said the policy was fair as B40 groups continued to require assistance amid rising living costs. Similarly, Zubaidah Zakaria said she supported the removal of subsidies for T20 groups in the interest of fairness.

items.title: Court extends remand of 24‑year‑old suspect in Jalan Ampang crash that killed couple
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — The driver in the fatal Jalan Ampang crash that killed a married couple on May 2 has had his remand extended to May 15, starting today. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the extension order was granted by Kuala Lumpur High Court senior assistant registrar Munirah Wan Mustafa. “The investigation papers will be referred to the Kuala Lumpur deputy public prosecutor’s office this afternoon for further instructions,” Mohd Zamzuri said in a statement today.

items.title: Bersatu sacks two more members, suspends three others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has sacked two more members and suspended three others, days after the party took similar action against several elected representatives and leaders. Those sacked were Kelantan deputy speaker Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi, who is also the Kok Lanas assemblyman, and Armada executive council member Wan Meor Safwat Naqiuddin Shamsudin. Meanwhile, Mersing member of parliament Muhammad Islahuddin Abas, Bukit Bunga assemblyman Mohd Almidi Jaafar and Armada executive council member Mohamad Arib Abu Kasim were suspended.

items.title: Police seek lorry driver after 65‑year‑old man killed in Seri Kembangan hit‑and‑run
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Police are looking for a lorry driver and witnesses to help investigate a hit-and-run accident that killed a 65-year-old man in Jalan Desa Serdang, Seri Kembangan, last Saturday. Kajang district police said in a statement that the incident happened at 9pm and involved a white bonded lorry and a Honda Wave motorcycle ridden by the victim. The victim was pronounced dead yesterday from severe head injuries.

items.title: UK's Starmer vows to prove doubters wrong and stay in power
items.summary: LONDON: Prime Minister Keir Starmer sought to quell a mounting rebellion in his party on Monday by vowing to prove the doubters wrong and stay in power to avoid plunging Britain into a new political crisis. A growing number of Labour lawmakers have turned on Starmer after his party suffered the worst local election results for a governing party in more than three decades last week, prompting a former junior minister to threaten to seek a leadership contest if he fails to offer radical change. Starmer sought to show he had got the message, saying in an impassioned speech that "incremental change won't cut it" in a country that has suffered two decades of economic stagnation and mounting social tensions.

items.title: Trump dismisses Iran's reply to peace plan, oil jumps as Hormuz closure persists
items.summary: DUBAI/WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump's swift rejection of Iran's response to a US peace proposal sent oil prices higher on Monday amid concerns the 10-week-old conflict will drag on, keeping shipping through the Strait of Hormuz paralysed. Teheran also included a demand for compensation for war damage and emphasised Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state TV said. Within hours, Trump dismissed Iran's proposal with a post on social media.

items.title: Late selling pushes Bursa Malaysia into negative territory at close
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Late selling pressure dragged Bursa Malaysia into negative territory at the close, reversing earlier gains as profit-taking in heavyweight banking and transportation counters dampen overall market sentiment. At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 2.75 points to 1,745.31 from Friday's close of 1,748.06. Turnover increased to 4.20 billion units worth RM3.17 billion compared with 3.31 billion units worth RM3.00 billion on Friday.

items.title: China says it won't allow Taiwan to attend WHO's annual assembly
items.summary: BEIJING/TAIPEI: China said on Monday it would not allow Taiwan to take part in the annual assembly of the World Health Organisation (WHO), which starts next week, as Taiwan said it would send a delegation anyway for meetings outside the actual event. Taiwan attended the WHO's World Health Assembly (WHA) as an observer from 2009 to 2016 under the administration of then-President Ma Ying-jeou, who signed landmark trade and tourism agreements with China. But Beijing began blocking Taiwan's participation in 2017, after then-President Tsai Ing-wen won office, over her refusal to agree to China's position that both China and Taiwan are part of "one China".

items.title: Fuel subsidy should remain for all Malaysians, says Penang opposition chief
items.summary: GEORGETOWN: Penang opposition chief Muhammad Fauzi Yusoff believes that the government should maintain fuel subsidies across the board regardless of income group. He said the government should consider a uniform and comprehensive subsidy approach, describing the retargeting of fuel subsidies involving the T20 groups as technically complex and potentially susceptible to manipulation at the implementation level. I think that is fairer," he said.

items.title: Malaysia manufacturing momentum strong despite mining drag, growth outlook lifted
items.summary: Kenanga Research has upgraded its 2026 manufacturing IPI growth projection following stronger-than-expected production performance in the first quarter of 2026 (Q1), a momentum expected to continue into the second quarter (Q2). The firm revised its forecast upward to 4.3 per cent from the earlier estimate of 3.5 per cent. Both firms maintained their 2026 gross domestic product growth forecast at 4.5 per cent, although Kenanga Research raised its first-quarter growth estimate to 5.1 per cent from 4.7 per cent, citing stronger manufacturing momentum.

items.title: Johor polls not discussed during audience with King, Regent, says Zahid
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 11 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said his audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Agong Sultan Ibrahim and Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail at Istana Besar here today did not involve discussions on the upcoming Johor state election. He said the discussions mainly centred on Umno’s 80th anniversary celebrations, which were held here today. Also present were Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and the party’s top leadership.

items.title: GOF Central Brigade arrested 335 migrants, 37 smugglers this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: General Operations Force Central Brigade policemen arrested 335 undocumented migrants and 37 suspected smugglers in operations against people smuggling between Jan 1 and May 11. "The migrants arrested comprised 237 adult men, 96 adult women and two boys. He said the 37 comprised five Malaysian men, 29 Indonesian men, two Indonesian women and one Myanmar man.

items.title: 'No home left' for Gazans stranded in West Bank since October 7
items.summary: UNDER the bleachers of a West Bank stadium, a dozen men from Gaza live in a former changing room, blocked from returning home by the war that erupted more than two and a half years ago. "I entered (Israel) only four days before the war," he said from the little space he had set up under the stands of Nablus city stadium. Then the war happened," he added, referring to the devastating Gaza war triggered by Hamas's Oct 7, 2023 attack on Israel.

items.title: Philippine lawmakers start vote on impeachment of presidential hopeful Duterte
items.summary: MANILA: Lawmakers in the Philippines started voting on Monday on whether to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, in what could create a major hurdle in her bid to run for the presidency in 2028. A House justice committee last month found probable cause for her impeachment in a petition from activists accusing her of misusing public funds, accumulating unexplained wealth and threatening the lives of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr, his wife and the former House speaker. Duterte faces removal from office and a ban from politics if convicted.

items.title: Dryer, hotter weather incoming: Southwest monsoon to begin May 14, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — The Southwest Monsoon is expected to begin on May 14 and will persist until September, following consistent winds blowing from the southwest, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) announced today. MetMalaysia director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said that during this period, low humidity levels will typically result in reduced cloud formation over most areas. However, Dr Mohd Hisham cautioned that heavy rain accompanied by strong winds and thunderstorms may still occur in certain areas.

items.title: EPF introduces i-Legasi, i-Emas and retirement goal calculator to help Malaysians plan for longer golden years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) today introduced the i-Legasi facility, which allows members to transfer a portion of their retirement savings to the EPF accounts of immediate family members. Other initiatives announced by the EPF include the rebranding of monthly EPF withdrawals for contributors aged 55 or 60 as i-Emas, as well as the introduction of a Retirement Goal Calculator. EPF chief executive officer Ahmad Zulqarnain Onn said more than 21,000 members have opted for monthly withdrawals after reaching the age of 55 or 60, reflecting growing awareness of the need for a structured monthly income after retirement.

items.title: Iran describes its proposal to end war with US as legitimate, generous
items.summary: DUBAI: Iran's proposal to end war with the US and reopen the Strait of Hormuz was legitimate and generous, Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said on Monday, adding that the US continues to uphold unreasonable and one-sided demands. "Our demand is legitimate: demanding an end to the war, lifting the (US) blockade and piracy, and releasing Iranian assets that have been unjustly frozen in banks due to US pressure," Baghaei said. The only thing we demanded was Iran's legitimate rights," he said.

items.title: Sarawak's push into aerospace sector can drive new economic growth
items.summary: KUCHING: Sarawak's strategic move to venture into the aerospace and high-technology sectors has the potential to open a new chapter in the state's economic development, said Sarawak Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar. "With adequate education and training, I believe Sarawakians can compete internationally and position Sarawak as a respected high-tech and aerospace hub in the region," he said when opening the First Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 19th Sarawak State Assembly today. "I hope AirBorneo will improve flight accessibility to Sarawak, including the state's interior areas.

items.title: China starts building world's largest ice-snow text base for intelligent connected NEVs
items.summary: BEIJING: China Automotive Technology and Research Centre Co, Ltd. (CATARC) has started construction of an ice-snow test base for intelligent connected new energy vehicles (ICNEVs), the world's largest of its kind, in north China's Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region. The new test base represents a leap in China's cold-weather NEV testing capabilities. Spanning approximately 1,000 mu (about 67 hectares), the test base will feature core functional zones covering calibration of NEV three-electric systems, namely battery, motor, electronic control, chassis systems, simulated snowfall testing for intelligent connected vehicles, and flying car evaluations.

items.title: T20 fuel subsidy review unfair to taxpayers, says Tuan Ibrahim
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional has described the proposal for a more targeted subsidy programme, particularly the rationalisation of RON95 fuel subsidies for the T20 income group, as unfair. Its deputy chairman, Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, said the government must clearly define what constitutes the "rich" category before implementing any policy changes. Tuan Ibrahim, who is also Pas deputy president, said the proposal reflected the government's failure to fulfil its earlier promises on easing the people's cost of living burden.

items.title: Umno marks 80th anniversary at Johor palace where party was founded, King joins commemoration
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 11 — Umno marked the 80th anniversary of its founding at Istana Besar Johor today, in a symbolic tribute to the party’s historic beginnings. Umno president and Deputy Prime Minister Ahmad Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi was among those present at the commemorative event. Also attending were deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, Johor Umno chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and other senior party leaders.

items.title: Police arrest Proton Inspira driver after viral three-vehicle crash on NKVE
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Police have arrested a 26-year-old Proton Inspira driver following a three-vehicle collision on the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) that went viral on social media, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the accident occurred at about 11pm on May 9 at Km28 of the highway heading towards the Duta toll plaza. “The impact caused the vehicles to veer into the left road barrier, resulting in heavy damage to all three vehicles,” he said in a statement cited by FMT.

items.title: Penang DAP Kampung Kolam branch first to declare Anthony Loke welcome to contest in the state
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Kampung Kolam DAP branch has passed a resolution to welcome and support party secretary-general Anthony Loke to contest in Penang, marking the first time a branch in the state has openly made such a call. Ng said the resolution was made with the aspiration of leading Penang towards greater progress. He noted that while Penang has always been a frontline state for DAP, which has never abandoned its goal of making the state a political fortress, the current state leadership is increasingly plagued by issues such as uneven leadership quality, factional infighting, and money-driven political campaigns.

items.title: Bangsar shooting case: Lorry driver charged with attempted murder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — A lorry driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of attempting to murder a man during a shooting incident outside an entertainment centre in Bangsar last month. If the act causes injury, the accused may face up to 20 years’ imprisonment. Therefore, I object to the bail application and request the court not to grant bail,” he said.

items.title: Six new senators sworn-in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 —Thirteen members of the Senate, including six new appointees, took their oath of office today before the Senate president, Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah. The six new appointees are acting Bentong Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohamad Zaini Salleh, Wanita MCA chief Datuk Wong You Fong, Sabah DAP chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe, Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Sarawak DAP assistant secretary Larry Asap and R.Thiagarajah. Meanwhile, six senators were reappointed, namely Tan Sri Low Kian Chuan, Datuk Seri Profesor Dr Awang Sariyan, Datuk Dr Mohd Hatta Md Ramli, Isaiah D. Jacob, Mohd Hasbie Muda and Abun Sui Anyitm.

items.title: Penang opposition leader alleges Chief Minister Incorporated accounts not fully audited
items.summary: He said CMI had failed to present its complete and audited accounts. “The accounts were also not tabled in the state legislative assembly even though CMI manages the state revenue and multi-million ringgit projects,” he said when debating the Penang Yang DiPertua Negri’s speech at the state legislative assembly today. The Sungai Dua assemblyman questioned the transparency of the state’s revenue management under CMI.

items.title: Penang tightens entry-point health security despite low hantavirus risk in Malaysia
items.summary: State exco for health, youth and sports Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said steps have been taken to tighten international entry screening measures. However the state health department has continued to implement health security protocols. “We have health officers stationed at international entry points including at the ports,” he said.

items.title: Sabah revises tourism strategy after airline route suspensions affect Kota Kinabalu connectivity
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 11 — Sabah is reviewing its tourism targets and adopting a more flexible strategy following a series of airline route suspensions affecting connectivity into the state capital. “We are already seeing these changes impacting airline capacity, with route suspensions and reduced frequencies affecting connectivity into Kota Kinabalu, and we are closely monitoring developments to ensure our tourism planning remains responsive,” he said in a statement. Among the affected routes are AirAsia’s temporary suspension of its Singapore–Kota Kinabalu route from May 13 to November 30, Jakarta–Kota Kinabalu from April 16 to May 21, and Ho Chi Minh–Kota Kinabalu beginning July 1.

items.title: Wood cutter remanded for suspected abuse of five-year-old stepson
items.summary: Acting Dungun police chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said the 36-year-old man was arrested yesterday and brought to the Magistrate's Court here today for a remand order. He said the arrest followed a police report lodged by the victim's 30-year-old biological mother at the Bukit Besi police station at 5.36 pm yesterday. Following that, Jumaidy said the victim, who attends a kindergarten in Bukit Besi, was taken to Dungun Hospital for examination at 8 pm yesterday.

items.title: Hit-and-run at Borneo Marathon leaves runner, Rela personnel injured; organisers say police probing incident
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 11 — Organisers of the Borneo International Marathon 2026 have confirmed a hit-and-run incident during the race, which left a 40-year-old participant and a Rela personnel injured. The organisers also reminded motorists to respect road closures and safety measures during organised sporting events. We urge all road users to respect road closures, marathon safety measures, and the presence of runners and crews during organised sporting events,” they said.

items.title: Customs seizes illicit cash, drugs and liquor at KLIA
items.summary: SEPANG, May 11 — Customs officers at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) have foiled attempts to smuggle out cash and drugs, and seized smuggled liquor, with a total value of nearly RM1.12 million in four separate cases last month. KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said that in the first case on April 2, the department, in collaboration with the Aviation Security (AVSEC) unit of Malaysia Airports, detained an Indonesian traveller at the Satellite Building, Terminal 1, for allegedly attempting to take RM129,500 in cash out of the country without declaration. “The case is being investigated under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 and Section 74(1)(d) of the Excise Act 1976,” he said.

items.title: Penang urged to clarify REE policy amid concerns over defence-linked use and InvestPenang governance
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government has been urged to take a clear stance to ensure that rare earth elements (REE) from the state are not used for weapons-related purposes. He said that last month, 57 activist groups in Malaysia raised concerns over the alleged supply of REE to the United States Department of Defense (Pentagon). He added that environmental activists and anti-war groups had also raised concerns that REE from Malaysia could be used for weapons-related purposes.

items.title: Two supervisors killed after being flung from car in Second Link five-vehicle crash
items.summary: Iskandar Puteri police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the accident occurred at about 5.40am on the highway towards Senai after the victim’s car skidded and lost control. “The victim’s car then hit a road divider before causing a motorcycle to lose control and crash into the rear,” he said in a statement today. “The car’s second victim, a 34-year-old man, was thrown out of the car due to the impact and fell under the overhead bridge,” he said, adding that the victim was also pronounced dead at the scene.

items.title: Penang CM: Rising costs will not delay Penang’s strategic water projects
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government will continue with its water supply projects under the Water Contingency Plan 2030 amid rising operational and construction costs due to global economic uncertainties. Penang chief minister Chow Kon Yeow said the projects implemented by the Perbadanan Bekalan Air Pulau Pinang (PBAPP) are crucial to ensuring the stability of the state’s water supply. Chow also said the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) is postponing several projects for at least six months.

items.title: Penang sees surge in medical tourists, revenue crosses RM1.1b in 2025
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — Penang recorded strong growth in its medical tourism sector, with foreign patient numbers rising to 527,176 in 2025. State executive councillor for tourism and creative economy Wong Hon Wai said this represents a 25.94 per cent increase compared to the previous year. He said data from the Penang Centre of Medical Tourism (PMED), covering 16 private hospitals in the state, showed foreign patient numbers stood at 418,608 in 2024.

items.title: Nearly 4m-long crocodile caught by Sibu licensed team, one more croc escapes
items.summary: SIBU, May 11 — A Sibu licensed crocodile catching team captured a male crocodile measuring around 13 feet (3.96 metres) in length and weighing 280kg in the waters of Kampung Sebenak, Daro on Saturday. Team leader Jeferry Siew said the operation followed frequent sightings of crocodiles in the area, raising concern among residents. He said they had initially planned a two-week operation but within days, two crocodiles were caught in traps.

items.title: Putrajaya weighs RON95 subsidy cuts, admits tough call on who should lose aid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 11 — The government has acknowledged the difficulty in determining which Malaysians should be excluded from RON95 fuel subsidies as it fine-tunes its targeted subsidy mechanism. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said one of the key challenges was setting a clear cut-off point for eligibility without affecting groups that still require assistance. He said the government is now studying implementation details, including where the threshold for eligibility should be set.

items.title: George Town gazettes 35 heritage items, eyes Unesco recognition for Penang traditions
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 11 — The Penang government has gazetted seven cultural heritage arts items and 28 traditional food items this year under the Penang State Heritage Enactment 2011. Wong said all intangible cultural heritage items gazetted at the state level may also be recommended for national recognition under the National Heritage Act 2005. In January, the state government announced the gazettement of 10 traditional foods and six intangible heritage items as state heritage.

items.title: Putrajaya names three Malaysian firms to spearhead Made-By-Malaysia chip project
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's administration today handed offers to three local firms picked to spearhead the ambitious made-in-Malaysia chip design project, hailing it as a significant step in strengthening the nation’s capabilities in integrated circuit design. Four tokens, including Arm CSS (Compute Subsystems) and Arm Flexible Access (AFA), were granted to Great Asic Technology Sdn. Akmal said the offer opens access to Arm’s computing platforms and intellectual property (IP) portfolio to accelerate the development of local semiconductor products designed in Malaysia.

items.title: Ex-aide to Teresa Kok claims trial to graft, cheating charges linked to school funding applications
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — A former aide to Seputeh MP Teresa Kok pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to two charges involving cheating and allegedly receiving RM500,000 in gratification linked to school smart board applications, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). FMT reported that Edmund Teoh, 40, was accused of deceiving Kok into believing that only two schools in her constituency had applied for funding for interactive smart boards, when in fact 16 schools had submitted applications. Teoh also faced a charge of allegedly receiving RM500,000 from one Ainuddin Aminuddin, 36, as an inducement to help secure smart board supply contracts for 16 schools in Seputeh through TMTIT Solutions, a company owned by Ainuddin’s wife.
